---
title: "About"
page-layout: article
toc: true
toc-title: "On this page"
---

::: {layout="[25,75]" .glass-card}

![](assets/img/monogram.svg){width="160px"}

<!-- TODO: replace with a professional headshot, e.g. ![](assets/img/photo.jpg){width="160px"} -->

<div>
**Raphael Ludwig**

Quantitative economist with five years at Vista Capital, where I helped build a macro
research desk from the ground up — covering Brazil and Europe, developing forecasting
and nowcasting models, and applying AI to research workflows. MSc in Economics from
PUC-Rio.

I'm currently looking for new opportunities in quantitative/macro research roles.
This site collects exercises that replicate and extend public empirical work — central
bank studies, academic papers, and sell-side research — as a demonstration of method.

[raphaludwig@gmail.com](mailto:raphaludwig@gmail.com) ·
[LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/raphaludwig/) <!-- TODO: confirm LinkedIn URL --> ·
[CV (PDF)](../cv/cv.pdf)
</div>

:::

## Experience

**Economist — Vista Capital** · Dec 2020 – May 2026

*Structuring the desk, data infrastructure and operational routines*

- Structured, alongside chief economist Alexandre Maia, the Macro Research area from its
  inception — defining processes, analytical routines, data infrastructure, and research
  products used daily by the fund.
- Built a proprietary macroeconomic data platform running on Docker on a Linux VM (later
  migrated to Prefect), with autonomous monitoring of updates, alerts, and automatic reports.
- Maintained 250+ internal routines for collecting, validating, and disseminating global
  data, focused on robustness, traceability, and fast reaction to releases.

*Modeling, quantitative research and applied AI*

- Developed and coordinated the architecture of macro projection models, including a
  proprietary model-selection algorithm and an automated short/medium-term forecasting
  system.
- Replicated and adapted empirical exercises from central banks, academic papers, policy
  blogs, and sell-side research reports.
- Built internal AI-based tools (using Claude Code), including dashboard/workflow
  automation, routine and algorithm optimization, and data infrastructure.

*Macro coverage, policy, and insight generation*

- Led Brazil coverage — combining data monitoring, quantitative modeling, macro-political
  scenario analysis, trips to Brasília, meetings with policy actors, and internal scenario
  discussions.
- Led Europe coverage — focused on the Euro Area and the UK, extending to Eastern Europe
  when relevant, integrating data, macro-political scenario, fiscal risk, inflation,
  activity, elections, and central banks.
- Led thematic coverage on topics with significant macro/market impact, including Covid-19
  and supply-chain disruption.
- Produced and presented weekly briefings on relevant macro topics; participated daily in
  the morning news call, responsible for the initial Brazil and Europe read.

*Training, technical leadership and talent development*

- Trained multiple intern cohorts in programming and macro topics, and collaborated on
  their applied research, including thesis supervision.
- Led internal trainings on economics, programming, automation, and applied AI — including
  a corporate crash course on AI and Claude Code usage.

## Education

**MSc in Economics — PUC-Rio** · Jan 2019 – Jul 2021
Dissertation: *"On the Missing Disinflation Puzzle: A Data-Driven Approach"*, advised by
Prof. Eduardo Zilberman and Prof. Marcelo Medeiros.

**BSc in Economics — PUC-Rio** · Jan 2013 – Jul 2017
Participated in the PET and PIBIC research programs with Prof. Pedro Souza (Queen Mary
University of London), focused on forecasting models and machine-learning applications.
Teaching assistant for *Programming Techniques for Economics II*, applied to
microeconomics, forecasting, and time series.
Undergraduate thesis: *"Nowcasting Unemployment with Google Trends: Evidence from the
Brazilian Labor Market"*, advised by Prof. Pedro Souza.

## Skills

**Quantitative macro research** — forecasting, nowcasting, short/medium-term models,
applied econometrics, machine learning, macro-political scenario analysis.

**Programming & data** — R, Python, MATLAB/Octave, Dynare, Git.

**Infrastructure & automation** — Docker, Linux/VMs, Prefect, Airflow, automated
pipelines, Slack integrations, Shiny, Streamlit, JavaScript.

**Applied research & AI** — replication of empirical exercises from central banks,
academic papers, and sell-side research; using Claude Code for automation, refactoring,
and routine optimization.

## Languages

Portuguese (native) · English (advanced) · German (advanced) · Spanish (basic)

## Beyond economics

Educated at Escola Alemã Corcovado (German School) through the German track, completing
the Abitur among the top three of the year. Federated volleyball player through
adolescence, team captain across several seasons. Ongoing interest in music, literature,
film, photography, and philosophy.
